learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate the communication and sales skills required for effective performance in the tourism and events industries.
Design a distribution strategy that considers the role of various entities in travel and events distribution systems.
Evaluate how tourism destinations and tourism and events businesses can use distribution channels and sales techniques to gain a competitive advantage.
Analyse the drivers of innovation and technological change and their impact on tourism destinations, businesses, events and travellers.

assessments.due_date:
17/08/2026 - 7/09/2026

Reflections and feedback to other group members to be handwritten and submitted to the teaching team before the end of the seminar each week.
assessments.task_description:
In the seminars in weeks 4-7 you will be practising your sales skills in a series of role plays. These role plays require at least three (3) active participants - an initiator; a prospect; an observer.

For each role play you will participate in all three (3) roles (initiator, prospect and observer). Each week you will submit two (2) handwritten documents to the teaching team before the end of the seminar:

  • The feedback you gave to the other members in your group. This can be dot points. You must submit this to the teaching team before your group members start writing their reflections.
  • Your role play reflection for one of the roles you played that week. You can only submit one reflection per week.

This is a pen-and-paper task to be completed in person on the paper provided during seminars and returned to the teaching team upon completion. The use of electronic devices or notes is not permitted while completing this task.  

This assessment will take place during that week's allocated seminar time and will be completed in class.

The design of the task (best 3 of 4 attempts) provides built-in flexibility. A practice session will be held in the seminar in Week 2.

Format

Pen and Paper/Handwritten and In-person during seminars. Accessible submission format is available if you are on a Student Access Plan (SAP). Please discuss this with the teaching team.

Word limit: 400-500 words, approximately 1 page.

More details will be discussed in class and provided in your Assessment Helper on Blackboard.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Choose an existing event or tourism organisation that you are familiar with and/or that you can access information about. Assume the role of sales representative for your chosen event or tourism organisation. Then identify a business to business (B2B) transaction that you will need to initiate as the sales representative for an event or tourism experience that your chosen event or tourism organisation manages. 

There are two (2) parts to your Sales Dialogue. Part A - prospecting process; and Part B - sales dialogue

Part A - Prospecting Process

As a short background to your sales dialogue, provide:

  • a brief description of your event or tourism organisation; your chosen event or tourism experience that your organisation manages; your prospect
  • an explanation of your strategic prospecting process (support with references as appropriate)

Part B - Sales Dialogue

Prepare a sales dialogue for your prospect. We will discuss the sales dialogue template in class.

Note: Your research will ONLY be based on an analysis of publicly available information. Therefore, you can NOT make any contact with your event or tourism business.

Further explanation of the assessment requirements is provided in your Assessment Helper.

Referencing: APA7

Word limit: 2500 words

Formatting:

  • font size 11 point
  • font type - Calibri; Times New Roman; Arial
  • line spacing - 1.5 spacing
assessments.submission_guidelines: Your Sales Dialogue must be submitted to Turnitin, which is accessed from Blackboard through the Assessment link.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
For Assessment 3 (Distribution Strategy), assume the role of marketing manager for the event or tourism organisation you identified in Assessment 2 (Sales Dialogue). As part of your role as marketing manager you are required to prepare a distribution strategy for the event or tourism experience you identified in Assessment 2 (Sales Dialogue). In your strategy:

  1. briefly describe your organisation and your event or tourism experience; (this can be an improved version from Assessment 2)
  2. briefly describe and justify your main target market; 
  3. identify three (3) distribution channels that could currently be used to connect your event or tourism experience to your target market. Use literature (academic and/or credible web based sources) to explain how each of your distribution channels functions; 
  4. depict your distribution channels as a distribution system; (diagram)
  5. conclude by using literature to explain how technological innovations could impact your distribution system in the future. 

Use academic references to support your work.

Note: Your research will ONLY be based on an analysis of publicly available information. Therefore, you can NOT make any contact with your event or tourism business.

Word limit: 3000-3500 words

Further details about how to prepare and present your Distribution Strategy will be discussed in class and provided in your Assessment Helper on Blackboard.

The marking rubric will be available on Blackboard.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Your Distribution Strategy must be submitted to Turnitin, which is accessed from Blackboard through the Assessment link.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 1: Introduction to Sales and Distribution

We start by considering what we are selling and how we want to sell it. Is our focus Business to Business (B2B) sales or Business to Consumer (B2C) sales? Should we focus on trust based selling or high pressure selling? Are we always selling or could we be buying? So is the focus on sales or negotiation, or are they one in the same? Sales and distribution in tourism and events is so much more than asking 'would you like fries with that?'.


Learning outcomes: L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 1: Introduction to Sales and Distribution We start by considering what we are selling and how we want to sell it. Is our focus Business to Business (B2B) sales or Business to Consumer (B2C) sales? Should we focus on trust based selling or high pressure selling? Are we always selling or could we be buying? So is the focus on sales or negotiation, or are they one in the same? Sales and distribution in tourism and events is so much more than asking 'would you like fries with that?'.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 2: Strategic Prospecting

For the first half of the semester our focus is on sales and negotiation. We need to start by understanding, and identifying, who we want to negotiate with. This is the first step in your Sales Dialogue (Assessment 2). We will also do a practice role play reflection to prepare you for Assessment 1 (Role Play Reflections).



Learning outcomes: L01, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 2: Strategic Prospecting For the first half of the semester our focus is on sales and negotiation. We need to start by understanding, and identifying, who we want to negotiate with. This is the first step in your Sales Dialogue (Assessment 2). We will also do a practice role play reflection to prepare you for Assessment 1 ( Role Play Reflections ).

learning_activities.topic:
Week 3: Business communication across cultures

Tourism and events operate in a global market. Understanding how to communicate in this global market is critical for successful negotiations. This week we are joined by an international business communication consultant who will help us understand how we can be effective communicators in this global business environment.



Learning outcomes: L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 3: Business communication across cultures Tourism and events operate in a global market. Understanding how to communicate in this global market is critical for successful negotiations. This week we are joined by an international business communication consultant who will help us understand how we can be effective communicators in this global business environment.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 4: Types of sales dialogues

We know who we want to negotiate with, but how do we start the conversation? This week we consider different types of sales dialogues and how these influence the way we communicate. This is also our first Role Play Reflection assessment week (Assessment 1). In Role Play 1 you will practice your learnings from the past few weeks. This is an in-class assessment.


Learning outcomes: L01, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 4: Types of sales dialogues We know who we want to negotiate with, but how do we start the conversation? This week we consider different types of sales dialogues and how these influence the way we communicate. This is also our first Role Play Reflection assessment week (Assessment 1). In Role Play 1 you will practice your learnings from the past few weeks. This is an in-class assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 5: Planning a sales dialogue

By now it should be clear that preparation is key. This week we work through the first six sections of a Sales Dialogue Template. In Role Play 2 you build on your learnings and your reflections from last week's role play. This is an in-class assessment.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 5: Planning a sales dialogue By now it should be clear that preparation is key. This week we work through the first six sections of a Sales Dialogue Template. In Role Play 2 you build on your learnings and your reflections from last week's role play. This is an in-class assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 6: Communication skills

Now that we know how to start the conversation, how do we elicit the information we need? Developing effective listening skills and questioning techniques is paramount. You will practice these skills in Role Play 3. This is an in-class assessment.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 6: Communication skills Now that we know how to start the conversation, how do we elicit the information we need? Developing effective listening skills and questioning techniques is paramount. You will practice these skills in Role Play 3. This is an in-class assessment.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 7: Handling objections and closing the sale

What do we do when we are faced with objections during a negotiation meeting? This week we think about how we prepare for objections and how to effectively end the meeting. You then practice these skills in Role Play 4. This is an in-class assessment. By now you should be confident to engage in a negotiation in a tourism or events business environment.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03
learning_activities.topic_title: Week 7: Handling objections and closing the sale
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 7: Handling objections and closing the sale What do we do when we are faced with objections during a negotiation meeting? This week we think about how we prepare for objections and how to effectively end the meeting. You then practice these skills in Role Play 4. This is an in-class assessment. By now you should be confident to engage in a negotiation in a tourism or events business environment.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 8: Travel Distribution - lessons from BCEC

Sales and negotiations are an important part of the broader process of selling tourism and events experiences. What are the distribution channels that can be used and how do we know which channels to use? This week our industry presenters from the Brisbane Convention and Exhibition Centre (BCEC) highlight the long term planning needed for an effective distribtution system.  

Learning outcomes: L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 8: Travel Distribution - lessons from BCEC Sales and negotiations are an important part of the broader process of selling tourism and events experiences. What are the distribution channels that can be used and how do we know which channels to use? This week our industry presenters from the Brisbane Convention and Exhibition Centre (BCEC) highlight the long term planning needed for an effective distribtution system.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 9: The Travel Distribution System

Last week we were introduced to the distribution channels used by the BCEC. This week we explore other distribution channels and intermediaries and how they relate to the broader Travel Distribution System. We also discuss the requirements for Assessment 3 (Distribution Report). 

Learning outcomes: L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 9: The Travel Distribution System Last week we were introduced to the distribution channels used by the BCEC. This week we explore other distribution channels and intermediaries and how they relate to the broader Travel Distribution System. We also discuss the requirements for Assessment 3 (Distribution Report).

learning_activities.topic:
Week 10: Assessment 3 Workbook

Interactive workbook to progress Assessment 3.

King’s Birthday Public Holiday - Monday 5th October 2026 - Check Blackboard for announcements about affected classes.

Learning outcomes: L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 10: Assessment 3 Workbook Interactive workbook to progress Assessment 3. King’s Birthday Public Holiday - Monday 5th October 2026 - Check Blackboard for announcements about affected classes.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 11: Distribution: Contemporary Distribution Channels

We will use case studies to consider two diverse intermediaries - travel agents and influencers. We explore the impact travel agents have on an organisation's ability to distribute their tourism experiences. Then we compare this with the pros and cons of utilising influencers as a distribution channel.

Learning outcomes: L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 11: Distribution: Contemporary Distribution Channels We will use case studies to consider two diverse intermediaries - travel agents and influencers. We explore the impact travel agents have on an organisation's ability to distribute their tourism experiences. Then we compare this with the pros and cons of utilising influencers as a distribution channel.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 12: Distribution: The Future

This week we explore if technology is enhancing or disrupting the Travel Distribution System. We consider topics like AI, big data and blockchain technology.

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Week 12: Distribution: The Future This week we explore if technology is enhancing or disrupting the Travel Distribution System. We consider topics like AI, big data and blockchain technology.

learning_activities.topic:
Week 13: Review and Consolidate

In our final week together we review and consolidate our learning over the semester as you finalise your Distribution Strategy (Assessment 3).

Learning outcomes: L02, L03, L04
